description: Letter in Ottoman Turkish to a state official regarding a rabbi's travels to Jerusalem. Dated: 20 Safar 1024 AH (21 March 1615 CE). In the introduction to her edition, Jane Hathaway notes: "This badly torn and creased document is a letter, composed in very florid Ottoman Turkish, to an Ottoman official regarding a rabbi (Ottoman Turkish, ḫāḫām) who is travelling to Jerusalem. The document in its present state provides little information about this rabbi, apart from his given name, Samuel (Şemvāl). He may have belonged to one of Cairo’s Jewish communities. The letter is dated 20 Ṣafar 1024 AH/21 March 1615 CE, which corresponds to 20 Adar II 5375 in the Hebrew calendar—i.e., late in the month preceding Nisan, when Passover falls. Thus Samuel was probably hoping to spend Passover in Jerusalem, as Jews from throughout the Ottoman Empire and beyond did each year.... Samuel probably carried this letter with him on his journey. This may explain why the space in line 1 for the official’s name and position are left blank, although it is possible they were written in above the line, as was common in this type of letter, and have not survived, since the letter’s top margin shows signs of tearing. Perhaps, however, the blank space was deliberate, allowing Samuel to insert the name, or to have the name inserted, of whatever official he might ask for aid in any locality en route. The letter asks the official to guarantee Samuel’s security along his route, lauding the addressee effusively, if rather formulaically, and appealing to his compassion. In line 5, the official is called ṣāḥib-i devlet (literally, ‘possessor of fortune’), which was usually an honorific applied to the grand vizier and provincial governors (see Ch. 2, Doc. 1, recto ll. 8, 10) [PGPID 20707]. Thus the intended recipient(s) may be the governors of districts along the overland route between Cairo and Jerusalem. Samuel may therefore have carried several copies of this letter, and the one that found its way to the Genizah may be one that he did not use. This could explain why the name and position of the recipient are not indicated." (Hathaway, Ottoman-Era Documents from the Cairo Genizah, 303-304)
description_he: מכתב בטורקית עות'מאנית לפקיד מדינה בנוגע לנסיעות רב לירושלים. תאריך: 20 ספר 1024 להיג'רה (21 במרץ 1615 לספירה).
